steps.title: Locate the median in each set
steps.content: Set A has $21$ values, so the median is the $11\text{th}$ value when sorted. The errant data value $19$ is the largest count and sits above the middle of the distribution.

steps.title: Compare after removing the 19
steps.content:
Set B has $20$ values, so its median is the average of the $10\text{th}$ and $11\text{th}$ values.

Because the removed $19$ is above the median position, the $10\text{th}$ and $11\text{th}$ values of set A are unchanged in set B, and they are equal — making the median of B equal to the median of A.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
